report_name: EU Commission Suffers Major Data Breach; TeamPCP Hackers Blamed for 92GB Data Heist

threat_actors.name: TeamPCP
threat_actors.description: A hacking group attributed with the data breach of the European Commission's AWS environment. The group is known for ransomware, data exfiltration, and cryptomining campaigns and leveraged a compromised version of the Trivy scanner to steal an AWS API key.

threat_actors.name: ShinyHunters
threat_actors.description: A hacking group that had previously claimed an attack on the EU, though its connection to the European Commission data breach by TeamPCP is still under investigation.

attack_patterns.name: Compromise Software Dependencies and Development Tools
attack_patterns.description: The European Commission was using a compromised version of the Trivy open-source vulnerability scanner, which was leveraged by attackers to steal a secret Amazon API key.

attack_patterns.name: Cloud Accounts
attack_patterns.description: Attackers used a stolen Amazon API key, obtained from a compromised version of Trivy, to gain unauthorized access to the European Commission's AWS account.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Cloud Storage
attack_patterns.description: Attackers exfiltrated approximately 92 gigabytes of compressed data, including a dataset of 52,000 files related to email communications, from the compromised AWS cloud storage environment.

tools.name: Trivy
tools.description: A compromised version of the Trivy open-source vulnerability scanner was used by the European Commission, which likely contained malicious code to steal an Amazon API key.

identities.name: European Commission
identities.description: Victim of a major data breach where 92GB of data was exfiltrated from its AWS environment after a compromised open-source tool led to the theft of an API key.

identities.name: CERT-EU
identities.description: The Computer Emergency Response Team for the EU institutions, which officially attributed the European Commission data breach to the TeamPCP hacking group.

identities.name: Amazon Web Services
identities.description: The cloud service provider whose environment, belonging to the European Commission, was compromised via a stolen API key.

identities.name: European Union Entities
identities.description: A class of 29 EU entities and 42 internal clients potentially affected by the data breach at the European Commission.

infrastructure.name: European Commission AWS Account
infrastructure.description: The compromised Amazon Web Services account from which 92GB of data was exfiltrated using a stolen API key.

indicators.name: Anomalous AWS S3 Data Access
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous AWS S3 Data Access
id: 7e5d1f8a-9c0b-4d2e-8f1a-3b6c8d7e5f1a
status: experimental
description: Detects AWS S3 'GetObject' API calls. A high volume of these events from a single user or an unusual user agent may indicate data exfiltration, as seen in the TeamPCP attack on the European Commission.
references:
  - https://therecord.media/eu-cyber-agency-data-breach-team-pcp
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-03
tags:
  - attack.t1530
  - attack.collection
level: informational
logsource:
  product: aws
  service: cloudtrail
detection:
  selection:
    eventName: 'GetObject'
    eventSource: 's3.amazonaws.com'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects AWS S3 'GetObject' API calls. A high volume of these events from a single user or an unusual user agent may indicate data exfiltration. This rule is intended for threat hunting and may require correlation to be effective.

indicators.name: Suspicious Trivy Scanner Execution
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Trivy Scanner Execution
id: b3c2d1a0-8e9f-4d1c-a0b9-e8f7d6c5b4a3
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of the 'trivy' open-source vulnerability scanner. A compromised version of this tool was used to steal credentials in the TeamPCP attack. Execution in unexpected contexts or with suspicious network connections should be investigated.
references:
  - https://therecord.media/eu-cyber-agency-data-breach-team-pcp
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-03
tags:
  - attack.t1195.001
  - attack.initial_access
level: low
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: linux
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '/trivy'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of the 'trivy' open-source vulnerability scanner. A compromised version of this tool was used to steal credentials in the TeamPCP attack.

indicators.name: Suspicious AWS GetSessionToken API Call
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious AWS GetSessionToken API Call
id: f1a0b9e8-d7c6-4b5a-9e8f-7d6c5b4a3b2c
status: experimental
description: Detects the AWS API call 'GetSessionToken', which can be used by attackers with a stolen static API key to generate temporary credentials. Frequent or unexpected usage should be investigated.
references:
  - https://therecord.media/eu-cyber-agency-data-breach-team-pcp
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-03
tags:
  - attack.t1078.004
  - attack.defense_evasion
level: medium
logsource:
  product: aws
  service: cloudtrail
detection:
  selection:
    eventName: 'GetSessionToken'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the AWS API call 'GetSessionToken', which can be used by attackers with a stolen static API key to generate temporary credentials. This was a key step in the TeamPCP attack chain.

relationships.target_name: Compromise Software Dependencies and Development Tools
Compromise Software Dependencies and Development Tools
